attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a chilled slice of potato in a sink, leaving the slicing knife in the fridge.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene depicts a kitchen environment with several objects relevant to the planning task. The key objects and their properties are as follows:

1. **Receptacles**:
   - **Sink**: A stainless steel sink with a faucet is embedded in the countertop. It is currently empty except for a red tomato and a fork resting inside it in some views. The sink is not openable and is not a container in the sense of being opened/closed, but it can hold objects (inReceptacle predicate applies). It is located centrally on the countertop.
   - **Fridge**: A black, double-door refrigerator with a bottom freezer drawer is visible. It is a receptacle and is openable. In the current state, it is closed (not opened). It is located to the left of the sink area.
   - **Microwave**: Not visible in any of the images; therefore, it is not present in the current scene state for this task.

2. **Objects**:
   - **Potato**: A single brown potato is visible on the countertop to the right of the sink in multiple images. It is an object (not a receptacle) and is currently whole (not sliced). It is not clean, not hot, and not cool.
   - **Knife**: A metallic knife is visible in the first image, lying on the countertop near the left edge. It is an object and is not currently held by any agent. It is not in any receptacle. It is the tool required for slicing.
   - **Tomato**: A red tomato is inside the sink in some views. It is an object and is not relevant to the current instruction.
   - **Fork**: A fork is also inside the sink with the tomato. It is an object and not relevant to the instruction.
   - **Bread loaf**: A loaf of bread is visible on the countertop to the right of the sink in some images. It is an object and not relevant to the instruction.

3. **Spatial Relationships**:
   - The potato is on the countertop, to the right of the sink.
   - The knife is on the countertop, to the left of the sink.
   - The tomato and fork are in the sink.
   - The fridge is to the left of the sink area and is closed.
   - The agent is not currently at any location (not atLocation any object).

4. **Current State**:
   - No object is being held by the agent (holdsAny is false).
   - The fridge is closed (opened is false).
   - The potato is not sliced, not clean, not hot, and not cool.
   - The knife is not in any receptacle and is not being held.
   - The sink contains the tomato and fork, but is otherwise available to receive the chilled potato slice.

5. **Visual Properties**:
   - The countertop is beige with a tiled backsplash.
   - The floor is tiled with large, light brown tiles.
   - The sink is metallic with a brushed finish.
   - The fridge is black with a glossy finish and has a handle on the freezer drawer.
   - The potato is brown and oval-shaped.
   - The knife is metallic with a straight blade and handle.
   - The tomato is bright red with a green stem.
   - The fork is metallic and has four tines.

The task requires the robot to obtain a potato, slice it with the knife, chill it in the fridge, and then place the chilled slice in the sink, while leaving the knife inside the fridge. The current scene provides all necessary objects in accessible locations, but none are in the required state for the final goal. The robot must first navigate to the potato or knife to begin the sequence of actions.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_task_problem)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        fridge1 - fridge
        sink1 - sink
        potato1 - object
        knife1 - knife
        tomato1 - object
        fork1 - object
        bread1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable fridge1)
        (not (opened fridge1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isSliced potato1))
        (not (isCool potato1))
        (not (isClean potato1))
        (not (isHot potato1))
        (not (isOn knife1))
        (not (isToggled knife1))
        (inReceptacle tomato1 sink1)
        (inReceptacle fork1 sink1)
        (atLocation agent1 potato1) ; Assuming agent starts near potato for simplicity, or this can be omitted if agent starts nowhere.
        ; Alternatively, if agent starts nowhere, omit the atLocation predicate and let GotoLocation be the first action.
        ; For this problem, we'll assume the agent must navigate, so we omit initial atLocation.
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle potato1 sink1)
        (isSliced potato1)
        (isCool potato1)
        (inReceptacle knife1 fridge1)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_task_problem)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        fridge1 - fridge
        sink1 - sink
        potato1 - object
        knife1 - knife
        tomato1 - object
        fork1 - object
        bread1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable fridge1)
        (not (opened fridge1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isSliced potato1))
        (not (isCool potato1))
        (not (isClean potato1))
        (not (isHot potato1))
        (not (isOn knife1))
        (not (isToggled knife1))
        (inReceptacle tomato1 sink1)
        (inReceptacle fork1 sink1)
        (atLocation agent1 potato1) ; Assuming agent starts near potato for simplicity, or this can be omitted if agent starts nowhere.
        ; Alternatively, if agent starts nowhere, omit the atLocation predicate and let GotoLocation be the first action.
        ; For this problem, we'll assume the agent must navigate, so we omit initial atLocation.
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle potato1 sink1)
        (isSliced potato1)
        (isCool potato1)
        (inReceptacle knife1 fridge1)
    ))
)
attempts.plan:
(pickupobject agent1 potato1)
(gotolocation agent1 fridge1)
(coolobject agent1 fridge1 potato1)
(gotolocation agent1 sink1)
(putobjectinreceptacle agent1 potato1 sink1)
(gotolocation agent1 knife1)
(pickupobject agent1 knife1)
(gotolocation agent1 potato1)
(sliceobject agent1 potato1 knife1)
(gotolocation agent1 fridge1)
(putobjectinreceptacle agent1 knife1 fridge1)
